summary: Heavily obfuscated JavaScript files in kecak256@1.0.5 require manual review
details: The package ships two heavily obfuscated JavaScript files (init.m.js and upchk.m.js) using hex-prefixed identifier mangling typical of automated obfuscators. Obfuscation alone is not proof of malicious behavior, but the package name resembles 'keccak256' (a popular cryptographic hashing library), and obfuscated code in a small utility-style package is an unusual combination that warrants human review. No traced evidence of credential theft, network exfiltration, install-time remote code execution, or lifecycle-hook abuse was confirmed in this analysis, so the disposition is review rather than block pending de-obfuscation and behavioral verification.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
